sutta: on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying in boga nagara at the ananda shrine then the exalted 1 addressed the mount saying monks yes lord replied those monks to the exalted 1 the exalted 1 said monks i will teach you these 4 great authorities do you listen to it carefully give your minds to it and i will speak the exalted 1 said this and what monks are the 4 great authorities in this case monks among might say face to face with the exalted 1 your reverence did i hear it face to face with him did i receive it this is dhamma this is vinaya this is the master's teaching [Music] i just stopped here for a moment from uh just to comment this dhamma vinaya is the master's teaching in the sutas you'll find that the buddha always refer to his teachings as dhamma vinaya and here you will get you will get to see what is as i've mentioned before the word tripitaka or tipitaka was never mentioned by the buddha in the sutas he always mentioned his teachings as dhamma vinaya now the buddha continued now monks the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorned but without welcoming without scorning the words and syllables thereof are to be closely scrutinized laid beside suta and compared with vinaya if when thus laid beside suta and compared with vinaya they lie not along with suta and agree not with vinaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is not the word of the exalted 1 arahan the fully enlightened 1 and it was wrongly taken by that monk so rejected monks here and again among might say face to face with the exalted 1 your reverence did i hear it face to face with him did i receive it this is dharma this is pinaya this is the master's teaching now the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorned but if when laid beside suta and compared with vinaya they lie along with suta and agree with vinaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is the word of the exalted 1 and it was rightly taken by that monk then bear this in mind as the first great authority then again among might say in such and such a monastery recites a sangha an order of monks together with an elder monk athera a leader face to face with that sangha i heard it face to face i received it this is dhamma this is vinaya this is the master's teaching now the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorned but if when laid beside suta and compared with vinaya they lie not along with suta and agree not with binaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is not the word of the exalted 1 and it was wrongly taken by that sangha of monks so do you reject it monks but on the other hand among might say in such and such a monastery recites a sangha of monks together with an elder among a leader face to face with that order i heard it face to face i received it this is dhamma this is vinaya this is the master's teaching now the words of that manga neither to be welcomed nor scorned this is but if when laid beside suta and compared with binaya those words lie along with suta and agree with vidnaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is the word of the exalted 1 it was rightly taken by that sangha of monks this monks is the second great authority to bear in mind yet again among might say in such and such a monastery recites a great number of elder monks widely learned versed in the doctrines who know dhammab by heart who know vinaya by heart who know the summaries by heart face to face with those elder monks i heard it face to face did i receive it this is dharma this is vinaya this is the master's teaching now the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorn but if when laid beside suta and compared with vinaya they lie not along with suta and agree not with binaya to this conclusion was yukum surely this is not the word of the exalted 1 it was wrongly taken by those elders so do you reject it monks herein again among might say in such and such a monastery recites a great number of elder monks widely learned versed in the doctrines who know dhamma by heart who no vinaya by heart who know the summaries by heart face to face with those elder monks i heard it face to face did i receive it this is dhamma this is the master's teaching now the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorn no but if when laid beside suta and compared with vinaya they line along with suta and agree with naya then to this conclusion must you come surely this is the word of that exalted 1 and it was rightly taken by those elders this monks is the third great authority here and again the monk might say in such and such a monastery recites a single elder monk of white learning versed in the doctrines who know who knows dhamma by heart who knows vinaya by heart who knows the summaries by heart face to face with that elder man i heard it face to face that i receive it this is dhamma this is vinaya this is the master's teaching now monks the words of that monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorn but without welcoming without scorning them the words and syllables thereof are to be closely scrutinized laid beside suta and compared with vinaya if when laid beside sultan compared with vinaya they lie not along with suta and agree not with vinaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is not the word of the exalted 1 the arahant the fully enlightened 1 and it was wrongly taken by that elder monk so do you reject it monks if if however a man should say in such and such a monastery recites a single elder monk of white learning verse in the doctrines who knows dhamma by heart who knows by heart etc and then the words of the monk are neither to be welcomed nor scorn and but to they should be laid beside sultan compared with vinaya um and if when laid beside compared with binaya they lie along with suta and agree with vinaya to this conclusion must you come surely this is the word of that exalted 1 the arahant the fully enlightened 1 and it was rightly taken by that elder monk this monks is the fourth great authority so these monks are the 4 great authorities that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the buddha is saying suppose a monk says he quotes like 4 different cases the first 1 is he says he such and such is the teaching of the buddha and he says the first 1 he says he heard it from the buddha himself if that is so then you must compare it with the sutas right and the vinaya and then the second case is if such if the monk says that he heard it from um from a sangha of monks with an elder monk athera and the whole sangha says that this is the buddha's teaching such and such is the buddha's teaching then it is to be compared with dhamma and vinaya and then the third 1 is if in a monk says that in a monastery there are a great number of terrors elder monks widely learned and they say that such and such is the buddha's teachings again it must be compared with suta and vinaya in and the fourth 1 is uh the monk says that in monastery there is a single elder monk who is widely learned and and he claims and such and such is the buddha's teachings then without welcoming or scorning what he says his words are to come to be compared with the suta and vinaya and after comparing with suta and vinaya then only you can come to the conclusion whether it is the buddha's teachings or not so here in this uttar you find is very clearly stated that dhamma means the sutas because when he claims this is dhamma it must be compared with the sutas and there is no mention of the abhidharma as we know it today in the original sutas of the buddha [Music] even though there are certain monks who claim that the dhamma includes the abhidharma in this uttar it is very clear that dhamma always refers to the sutas and vinaya refers to the few books of the vinayat pitaka which concerns monks the word tipitaka or tripitaka was never mentioned in the su tesla and it is a later uh word that came later i read to you the footnote here by the uh polytech society and the footnote here says that there were evidently only 2 collections pitakas but abhi dhamma is obviously a much later collection had there been any pitakas at the time of the composition of this suta the word would surely have been used in it so you see the word pitaka is never mentioned in the sutas as we know it today and the word tripitaka also never mentioned and the other thing um we should know is in the second sangha council which was held about 100 years after the parinibana of the buddha there were certain monks who claimed that they follow such and such a teaching because it was handed down to them from their teachers and so it was decided at the second sangha council that if a certain manga gets a teaching from his teacher then he should compare what his teacher teaches with the sutas and the vinaya and if it does not agree with the sutas and the vinaya then we should not follow the words of that teacher monk because our original teacher is the buddha that's why we take refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha so it is very clear here even nowadays whatever whatever senior monks teach even if they are very well known very respectable monks whatever they teach should be not taken on their word but compared with the sutas and the vinaya that is why the foundation in the sutas is very very important nowadays there is such a [Music] such a lot of writings on the buddha's teachings and so many different interpretations that is very dangerous to put your faith in a teacher it is better to put our faith in the original sutas of the buddha now you come to asuta
